name: Pancetta Stuffed Flounder with Boiled New Potatoes and Hollandaise
recipeIngredient: 2 cups day old white bread cubes (about 10 ounces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees.
2. In a large sauté pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
3. Render the pancetta until crispy, about 10 minutes. Remove and set aside.
4. Add onions and sauté until wilted, about 3-4 minutes. Stir in garlic and sauté for 1 minute.
5. Add bread cubes and cook until crisp. Remove from heat and transfer to a mixing bowl.
6. Bring milk to a boil, then stir in 2/3 of the milk into the bread mixture. Stir in beaten eggs and parsley. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Mix in flour to bind the stuffing if needed. Adjust with more milk if dry.
8. Stuff each flounder with 1/4 of the stuffing. Brush fish with melted butter and Essence, then season.
9. Place fish on a baking sheet. Bake for 15-20 minutes.
10. Serve potatoes first, followed by fish. Spoon Hollandaise over fish and around rim. Garnish with fried parsley and diced peppers.
Yield: 4 servings
